facet_level_map._doc: Ordinal facet_level -> concrete DSL primitive args. Engine MUST thread these into sphere(subdivisions=), cylinder/cone/tube/revolve(sections=), and rounded_box (which currently HARDCODES subdivisions=2 — patch it to accept a corner_subdiv arg).

realistic.aesthetic: True-to-life proportions and surface; minimal exaggeration, smooth high-res curves, crisp anatomical detail.
realistic.dsl_mapping: sphere(subdivisions=4) / ellipsoid(...,subdivisions=3); cylinder(sections=64); revolve(sections=64); rounded_box(radius=0.6). Build organic masses with loft/revolve profiles, not stacked primitives. Keep head_body_ratio near anatomical 0.25-0.33.

stylized_clean.aesthetic: Designer-toy clean: real proportions softened, smooth surfaces, generous fillets, no fine clutter. The safe default.
stylized_clean.dsl_mapping: sphere(subdivisions=3); rounded_box(radius=2.0); prefer hull() to fuse masses into one clean silhouette. eye = sphere(d = base_eye_d * 1.30).

cartoonish.aesthetic: Big-head, big-eye, bouncy and rounded. Exaggerated friendly features, soft fat fillets, simplified detail.
cartoonish.dsl_mapping: head sphere(d) sized so head_d/body_h ~= 0.85; eyes = sphere(d = base_eye_d * 1.8) raised as bosses, pupils carved with sphere difference; rounded_box(radius=3.5); cylinder(sections=16).

anime.aesthetic: Sleek and angular-elegant: tall slim body, large almond eyes, sharp jaw/spike accents, smooth skin but crisp silhouette edges.
anime.dsl_mapping: almond eyes = sphere(d=base*2.1) then .scale([1.0,0.55,1.3]) for the slanted oval; hair/spikes = cone or wedge; slim body via loft with tapered rings (limb_taper 1.25 = elongate Z). Keep some sharp edges (wedge/prism) against smooth masses.

chibi.aesthetic: Super-deformed: 1:1-ish head-to-body, tiny stubby limbs, huge eyes, maximum cuteness, almost no surface detail.
chibi.dsl_mapping: head sphere as large as body mass (ratio ~1.0); limbs = short capsule(h small) with limb_taper 0.55 (stubby); eyes = sphere(d=base*2.4). Fuse everything with hull()/union into a single rotund blob. fillet 4.0.

low_poly.aesthetic: Faceted crystalline look — deliberately few flat polygons, hard edges between facets, no smoothing.
low_poly.dsl_mapping: EVERY rounded mass MUST use the low facet args: sphere(subdivisions=0) = 20-face icosahedron; cylinder(sections=6) = 24 faces; cone(sections=6); prism(sides=6) for limbs/trunk. NEVER use rounded_box here (it hardcodes subdiv=2 -> 428 faces and breaks the look) — substitute box() or prism(). flat shading on render.

geometric.aesthetic: Abstract primitive-built: subject reduced to recognizable arrangement of clean platonic shapes (cubes, cylinders, cones), modern/minimal.
geometric.dsl_mapping: Compose the subject from a small set of distinct platonic solids kept VISUALLY SEPARATE (don't hull into one blob). box/cube for torso, cylinder(sections=8) for neck/limbs, cone for beak/ears, sphere(subdivisions=1) only for eyes. Minimal fillet 0.4 just to soften the print edge.

exaggeration_application_rules._doc: How an engine APPLIES the multipliers given a base proportional plan (e.g. from a reference guide).
exaggeration_application_rules.head_body_ratio: Target head_diameter = head_body_ratio * body_height. Scale the head primitive's d to hit this; reposition head atop body.
exaggeration_application_rules.eye_size_mult: eye_primitive_d = base_eye_d * eye_size_mult. Clamp so eye_d <= 0.55 * face_width to avoid eyes merging.
exaggeration_application_rules.feature_exaggeration: Multiply protrusion length of beaks/ears/noses/snouts (cone h, wedge depth) by this. Also multiplies eye_socket boss height.
exaggeration_application_rules.overall_roundness: 0=keep primitive edges, 1=maximally rounded. Engine blends: roundness>0.6 -> swap box->rounded_box and union via hull(); roundness<0.3 -> keep box/prism, no hull.
exaggeration_application_rules.limb_taper: >1 elongates limbs along their long axis (scale Z of capsule/cylinder); <1 shortens+fattens (stubby).
exaggeration_application_rules.surface_relief_mult: Multiplies the protrusion/inset depth of raised bosses and carved details (eye sockets, feather/scale relief).
exaggeration_application_rules.fillet_radius_mm: Direct value for rounded_box(radius=) and any chamfer; 0.0 disables (low_poly).
exaggeration_application_rules.detail_density: Scales COUNT of repeated decorative features (feathers, scales, buttons) via linear_pattern/circular_pattern count = round(base_count * detail_density).

print_safety_clamps._doc: After style is applied, clamp to print-readiness BEFORE export. Style never overrides physics.
print_safety_clamps.min_protrusion_mm: max(2.0, fillet_radius_mm) so thin exaggerated spikes (anime hair, ear tufts) don't fall below printable; thicken or fillet base.
print_safety_clamps.note: low_poly/geometric sharp bottom edges still need .on_bed(); add a 0.4mm base chamfer regardless of style edge_treatment.
